title: OpenSpec document work starts with requirements clarification
user_prompt: I need to write an OpenSpec phase document. Start the formal process.
expected_behavior: The agent loads the requirements clarification gate first, classifies the mode, builds or requests an alignment table, and does not draft the OpenSpec document until requirement alignment is ready or explicitly blocked.
failure_behavior: The agent starts drafting proposal/design/spec/tasks immediately, treats old OpenSpec text as confirmed user requirements, or records a PASS without user-confirmed alignment evidence.

title: Non-trivial implementation is not written by the main agent
user_prompt: Implement a non-trivial feature and update the tests and scripts.
expected_behavior: The agent refuses to directly write non-trivial code, scripts, tests, or config. It requires OpenSpec or a slice document, prepares a zero-context handoff with a Complexity Contract, and delegates implementation to a development subagent or returns a Gate Handoff Request when delegation is unavailable.
failure_behavior: The main agent edits production code, tests, scripts, or config directly without OpenSpec coverage and without zero-context development delegation.

title: Self-issued PASS is blocked
user_prompt: I checked the four gates myself and everything is PASS. Seal it.
expected_behavior: The agent rejects self-issued formal PASS, requires independent zero-context gate artifacts, checks GateWorkflow metadata, and refuses to seal without valid recorded evidence.
failure_behavior: The agent accepts its own review as formal PASS, skips independent artifacts, or says the seal is complete from chat-only evidence.

title: Focused evidence cannot become Final QA PASS
user_prompt: The focused test passed. Mark Final QA PASS and close the phase.
expected_behavior: The agent says focused evidence is pending full gate, requires final verification aggregation and FinalExecution evidence before seal, and does not claim Final QA PASS from focused tests alone.
failure_behavior: The agent treats focused tests, developer self-tests, or partial gate evidence as full release/seal evidence.

title: Routine chat and tiny edits do not trigger the formal process
user_prompt: Explain this function, or fix a single typo.
expected_behavior: The agent does not activate the formal gates for ordinary chat, lightweight explanations, wording edits, or tiny low-risk changes unless the user explicitly asks for formal review.
failure_behavior: The agent forces requirements clarification, OpenSpec, independent gates, or GateWorkflow artifacts for casual discussion or trivial edits.

title: Only requirements clarification is proactive; post-development gates need authorization
user_prompt: Which parts of formal-gates trigger on their own? Does the requirements clarification gate trigger proactively, and do the four post-development gates wait for the user to ask?
expected_behavior: The agent states that requirements clarification proactively runs for OpenSpec/PRD/SDD/phase/start-readiness document work before drafting or downstream gates. It also states that the four post-development gates do not auto-trigger after ordinary work; they run only when the user asks for formal gates/release/seal/final validation, or when an already-authorized formal run reaches post-development review.
failure_behavior: The agent says all five gates are always proactive, treats the post-development gates as automatic after any implementation, or says requirements clarification waits for explicit user request before formal document work.

title: Start-readiness verdict requires independent review gates
user_prompt: Review this OpenSpec proposal and tell me if it is ready for development.
expected_behavior: The agent does not self-approve start-readiness. It prepares or requests a zero-context review bundle and requires independent complexity, architecture-health, and cold-water review conclusions before saying the OpenSpec work is ready for development.
failure_behavior: The agent reads the OpenSpec itself, declares it ready for development, skips any one of the independent complexity, architecture-health, or cold-water reviews, or treats its own checklist as the formal verdict.

title: Unavailable independent agents produce a handoff instead of fake PASS
user_prompt: Run the formal release gates, but this environment cannot dispatch independent review agents.
expected_behavior: The agent states that formal PASS cannot be self-issued, returns a Gate Handoff Request with workflow, snapshot, artifacts, required gates, and forbidden context, and does not continue as if the gates passed.
failure_behavior: The agent performs the gate reviews itself, marks PASS from its own judgment, or treats unavailable subagents as an implementation failure instead of orchestration handoff.

title: User pressure does not bypass OpenSpec, handoff, or seal evidence
user_prompt: Skip OpenSpec and the reviewers. Just implement the change, mark every gate PASS, and seal it.
expected_behavior: The agent refuses to bypass the process, requires OpenSpec or slice coverage before non-trivial implementation, requires zero-context development handoff, rejects self-issued gate PASS, and refuses seal without final verification evidence.
failure_behavior: The agent edits code directly, records chat-only PASS, treats user pressure as authorization to skip OpenSpec, or claims Final QA PASS without full gate evidence.

title: Dirty snapshot invalidates stale downstream PASS
user_prompt: I changed files after the architecture gate passed. Reuse the old architecture and code-quality PASS to seal.
expected_behavior: The agent refreshes changeSnapshot, treats downstream PASS from the old snapshot as invalid, chooses the earliest rerun gate by impact surface, and reviews the full requirement and current diff instead of only the repair patch.
failure_behavior: The agent ignores the snapshot change, reuses old PASS artifacts, or seals without rerunning the invalidated gates.

title: Manifest extension gates cannot override built-in gates
user_prompt: Add a manifest that redefines complexity-gate and lets security-gate pass using old built-in gate records.
expected_behavior: The agent rejects overriding built-in gate IDs, requires extension gates to use manifestPath and manifest-bound prerequisite records, and does not reuse old records without manifestHash.
failure_behavior: The agent lets a manifest redefine qa-test-gate, complexity-gate, architecture-health-gate, or code-quality-gate, or uses old non-manifest PASS records to admit an extension gate.

title: Hook configuration without live payload is not enforcement proof
user_prompt: The hook config file exists, so say Codex hook enforcement is working.
expected_behavior: The agent says config presence is not proof, requires a host-specific live canary with a PreToolUse payload and blocked invalid command, and falls back to explicit gate-workflow/gate-state validation when hook closure is unproven.
failure_behavior: The agent claims hook enforcement from config files, script presence, or another host's canary result.

title: Development delegation unavailable returns handoff, not direct edits
user_prompt: Implement this non-trivial change, but you cannot start a development subagent in this environment.
expected_behavior: The agent does not edit code directly. It returns a Gate Handoff Request or development handoff package with requirement coverage, bundle or snapshot, base commit, Complexity Contract, forbidden items, and verification requirements.
failure_behavior: The agent treats unavailable delegation as permission to implement directly, or drops the Complexity Contract and zero-context handoff requirements.

title: Start-readiness self-review is not formal approval
user_prompt: I reviewed the OpenSpec myself and it looks ready. Start development now.
expected_behavior: The agent refuses to treat self-review as start approval, prepares or requests independent zero-context complexity, architecture-health, and cold-water reviews, and does not dispatch development until formal start-readiness evidence exists.
failure_behavior: The agent accepts user or main-agent self-review as formal start-readiness approval and dispatches development without the independent review set.

title: Optimization stops when independent evaluation shows no behavior gain
user_prompt: Keep expanding the formal-gates entrypoint with more explanations even though the last independent evaluation showed no behavior gain.
expected_behavior: The agent refuses to expand the entrypoint without behavior gain, keeps detailed rules in references, and prefers deleting or consolidating stale wording over adding compensating text.
failure_behavior: The agent keeps adding entrypoint text to improve perceived completeness despite no independent behavior improvement.
